claim: Recomputing all 1162 Trans candidates from T2A-DEGREE42-SEARCH at dps=150, K_1500 yields 1162/1162 convergent with |K_1500-K_1499|<1e-40; 0 diverged, 0 errors.

At dps=150, tol=1e-100, hmax=1e12, none (0/1162) of the (4,2) Trans candidates satisfies a PSLQ relation in the bilinear-pi basis {1, L, pi, L*pi, pi^2, L*pi^2, log2, L*log2, zeta3}; all 1162 are 'trans-hard' under this F(2,4)-style basis.

After removing zeta(2)=pi^2/6 and zeta(4)=pi^4/90 from the extended basis to eliminate spurious basis-internal relations, none (0/1162) of the trans-hard families satisfies a PSLQ relation with nonzero L-coefficient in {1, pi, pi^2, pi^3, pi^4, zeta3, log2, log3, sqrt2, sqrt3, euler-mascheroni}; the four 'pi^2 hits' produced by the un-pruned basis were the trivial identity pi^2-6*zeta(2)=0 with L-coefficient zero.

All 1162 (4,2) trans-hard families at CMAX=1 share ratio a4/b2^2 = 1.0 exactly; this is a trivial consequence of the CMAX=1 bound (a4 in {1}, b2 in {-1,+1}) and is NOT independent evidence of an Apery-like structural law; analogous F(2,4) ratio invariants (-2/9 or 1/4) require CMAX>=2 testing for a comparable claim at degree-(4,2).
